name: Buseo Reservoir and Barranco de la Hoz Loop
name_original: El Barranco de la Hoz — Circular desde el Embalse de Buseo
description: Hike the moderate 9.1-mile Ravine of La Hoz circular trail from Buseo Reservoir, featuring waterfalls and stunning gorge views.
region_intro: Hiking around Chera offers diverse landscapes characterized by natural parks, reservoirs, and varied terrain. The region features a mix of hills, ravines, and water bodies, providing a range of outdoor experiences. Trails often traverse through natural park areas, offering opportunities to explore local flora and geological formations. This area is suitable for hikers seeking routes with moderate elevation changes and scenic views. Best hiking trails around Chera The most popular hiking route is Buseo Reservoir and Barranco de la Hoz Loop, a 9.1 miles (14.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 27 minutes to complete. This route explores the Buseo Reservoir and the Barranco de la Hoz ravine. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pico Ropé – Trail with views loop from Chera, a moderate 8.6 miles (13.8 km) path. This trail ascends to Pico Ropé, offering panoramic views of the surrounding landscape. Local hikers also love the Buseo Reservoir – Barrio Fountain loop from Chera, a 7.8 miles (12.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain around the Buseo Reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 40 minutes. Hiking around Chera is defined by reservoir paths, hill climbs, and natural park trails. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easier walks to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Chera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 160 hikers have used komoot to explore Chera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Chera?
A: Chera offers a diverse network of over 19 hiking routes. These trails range from easy walks to more challenging moderate and difficult routes, catering to various experience levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking in Chera?
A: Hiking around Chera features diverse landscapes, including natural parks, reservoirs, hills, ravines, and water bodies. You'll find a mix of terrain with moderate elevation changes and scenic views, often traversing through natural park areas.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Chera?
A: Yes, Chera offers at least one easy hiking route. While most trails are moderate, there are options for those seeking a less strenuous experience. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38253147" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buseo Reservoir – Buseo Reservoir loop from Río Reatillo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route with relatively low elevation gain, making it accessible for many.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Chera?
A: Many of the popular routes in Chera are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e816949466/buseo-reservoir-and-barranco-de-la-hoz-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buseo Reservoir and Barranco de la Hoz Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10376525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buseo Reservoir – Barrio Fountain loop from Chera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the notable natural features or landmarks to see while hiking in Chera?
A: Chera's hiking trails often lead to beautiful natural features. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5137250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buseo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which is a central feature for many routes. Other highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3028573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco Azul of Chulilla&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/600621" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loriguilla Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering panoramic views in Chera?
A: Yes, Chera offers several excellent viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10376468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Ropé – Trail with views loop from Chera&lt;/a&gt; ascends to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5137351" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Ropé&lt;/a&gt;, providing panoramic vistas. Other notable viewpoints include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/600629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Loriguilla Reservoir from the El Fandarín viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4579981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Frailecico Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is Chera a good destination for family-friendly hikes?
A: Chera's varied terrain includes options suitable for families. While many routes are moderate, there are easier sections and shorter loops, especially around the reservoirs, that can be enjoyable for families. Always check the specific trail details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Chera?
A: Generally, many natural areas in Spain are dog-friendly, especially on trails, but specific rules can vary by natural park or protected area. It's always best to check local signage or the regulations for the Parque Natural de Chera-Sot de Chera regarding dogs, especially if they need to be on a leash.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Chera?
A: The hiking experience in Chera is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the scenic reservoir paths, and the rewarding hill climbs that offer beautiful views of the surrounding landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in Chera?
A: Given the region's characteristics, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions in Chera, with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, especially in inland Spain, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable if hiking during this season. Winters are typically mild but can be cooler and wetter.

Q: Are there any specific peaks I can hike to in Chera?
A: Yes, you can hike to several peaks in the Chera area. &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5137351" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Ropé&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent summit accessible via a dedicated loop trail. Other peaks include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2715434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Gordo Summit&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3251984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Summit of Pantanillo Hill above Siete Aguas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How long do the hikes in Chera typically take?
A: The duration of hikes in Chera varies depending on the route and your pace. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e816949466/buseo-reservoir-and-barranco-de-la-hoz-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buseo Reservoir and Barranco de la Hoz Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 9.1 miles (14.7 km), typically takes around 4 hours and 27 minutes to complete. Shorter routes will take less time, while more challenging ones may take longer.
tips: Nice pool with a waterfall over the La Hoz ravine. In times of rain you can see it in all its splendor, although it can be somewhat difficult to reach it.
Nice waterfall over the ravine, one of the most picturesque points of the place. Getting there can be a bit complicated in times of rain and depending on the water that runs through the ravine.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access The Ravine of La Hoz circular trail?
faqs.answer: The circular route typically starts and ends near the Buseo Reservoir. You can find designated parking areas close to the reservoir, which serve as convenient starting points for the hike.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail is generally accessible, it's important to check specific regulations for pets within the Chera-Sot de Chera Natural Park. If dogs are permitted, they should always be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable in various seasons, but it's particularly spectacular after rainfall when the waterfalls, like the Chorrero de la Castellana, are at their fullest and the water in the ravine displays a vibrant turquoise hue. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for hiking.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail or to enter the Chera-Sot de Chera Natural Park.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse and considered moderate. You'll encounter a mix of shaded areas, open stretches, pine forests, and rugged, rocky sections. There are well-constructed paths, bridges, stairs, and even metal walkways in certain areas to navigate the more challenging parts of the ravine. Some sections may be muddy after rain.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy stunning views of the Buseo Reservoir and the surrounding mountains. Key highlights include the dramatic Barranco de la Hoz gorge, interesting rock formations, and the impressive Chorrero de la Castellana waterfall. You'll also pass by the Waterfall and Pool at Barranco de la Hoz and another Waterfall on the Barranco de la Hoz. Keep an eye out for the natural Tufa Bridge on the Río Blanco.

faqs.question: Are there opportunities for swimming on this route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the area offers chances to swim in the Buseo Reservoir and in pools formed by nearby rivers and waterfalls within the Hoz Ravine itself, providing a refreshing break during your hike.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: The full circular route is rated as moderate and requires good fitness due to some steep sections and rocky paths. While some parts are easily accessible, the overall trail demands sure-footedness. It might be challenging for very young children or those new to hiking, but experienced families with good fitness could enjoy it.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations for hiking in the Chera-Sot de Chera Natural Park?
faqs.answer: As the trail is located within the Chera-Sot de Chera Natural Park, general park regulations apply. These typically include staying on marked paths, not disturbing wildlife, carrying out all trash, and being mindful of fire restrictions, especially during dry seasons. Always respect the natural environment.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, it's advisable to bring sturdy hiking boots, sufficient water, snacks, sun protection, and a first-aid kit. If hiking after rain, be prepared for muddy sections. A camera is also recommended to capture the stunning scenery.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Embalse de Buseo, Embalse de Buseo, Reatillo y La Garita, and another section named Embalse de Buseo.
